findings.title: sk-code BLOCKED-BY-ROUTE-GOLD is pre-existing and orthogonal to compiled routing
findings.evidence: Directly verified: all 23 of sk-code's eligible compiled-routing-parity rows have compiledGoldPass === legacyGoldPass (byte-for-byte identical pass/fail pattern, including all 10 failing rows: SD-001, SD-002, LS-001..LS-004, CS-001, CS-003, CS-005, CS-007). Compiled routing reproduces legacy's behavior exactly, including legacy's own pre-existing route-gold gaps. Corroborates operator memory: packet 056 'OPEN operator item: sk-code empty-gold + CI mode-a-gate before 028->main'; packet 124 'sk-code Lane-C re-baseline' handoff.
findings.action: None required by this task (verification-only, no fixes in scope). Tracked separately by the operator.

findings.title: 17 vitest failures in model-benchmark/shared lanes -- confirmed pre-existing and unrelated to compiled routing
evidence: grep of all 6 failing test files for compiled-routing/DEFAULT_ON_HUBS/resolve.cjs/compiled-route keywords: zero matches.

Root cause 1 (13 of 17 failures, sweep-isolation.vitest.ts + downstream status-code failures in optin-scorer.vitest.ts, run-benchmark-hardening.vitest.ts, materialize-fixture-id.vitest.ts): the fixture directory .opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/assets/model-benchmark/benchmark-fixtures/ contains hyphen-named files (hard-merge-intervals.json, harder-semver-compare.json, validate-ipv4.json, ...) but the test files' string literals request underscore names (hard_merge_intervals.json, harder_semver_compare.json, validate_ipv4.json). git log on the failing test files shows the most recent commit as cc77a1e550a 'refactor(sk-doc): migrate filesystem names to kebab-case (020, squashed)' -- a repo-wide, unrelated filename-convention migration that renamed the assets without updating these literals.

Root cause 2 (sweep-foundation.vitest.ts, 0 tests / suite-level failure): ENOENT on .../sk-prompt/assets/framework-registry.json; the file now lives at .../sk-prompt/prompt-improve/assets/framework-registry.json following the separate, unrelated sk-prompt parent-hub merge (2026-07-09, 2667 files git-mv'd per operator memory).

Root cause 3 (remediation.vitest.ts, 1 test): expects dispatchModel.buildSpawnSpec('cli-opencode', ...) to throw /Unknown executor/ as a 'retired executor'; it does not throw, i.e. cli-opencode is still a live/supported executor kind in dispatch-model.cjs -- a stale test/implementation mismatch in the model-benchmark executor-allowlist, unrelated to skill routing.
findings.action: None required by this task (out of scope: model-benchmark lane, not compiled-routing). Flagged for the model-benchmark/sk-prompt owners separately.

findings.severity: real, actionable (test-suite staleness, not a routing-correctness regression)
findings.title: .opencode/bin/compiled-routing-foundation.vitest.ts has 3 failing tests that assert the PRE-flip (empty-cohort) behavior
findings.command: cd .opencode/.opencode && ... ; actually: cd .opencode && npx vitest run --config vitest.config.bin.ts
findings.result: 1 test file (8 files total in that run) failed: 3 of 24 tests in compiled-routing-foundation.vitest.ts failed; 61/64 passed in that file; other 7 files in the same config all passed.
failingTests: 'default-on cohort ships empty at both read sites' -- expects resolver.DEFAULT_ON_HUBS.size===0 and ADVISOR_DEFAULT_ON_HUBS.size===0; resolver (the runtime engine copy) is actually 7 post-flip.

'unset resolves to legacy (null) for every hub -- byte-identical default' -- expects resolveRoute(hub,...) to return null with no flag set; it now returns a real compiled decision for every hub (by design, post-flip).
'--all default state reports every hub as flag-off drift, none broken' -- expects every hub's status causeCode==='flag-off'; it is now 'compiled-serving' for every hub (by design, post-flip).
findings.rootCause: Commit 7dfffa0c93 (the default-on flip) updated .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs's DEFAULT_ON_HUBS from empty to all 7 hubs (correctly, and by design), but did not update this foundation-invariant test file's assertions to match the new intended default-on semantics. The routing behavior itself is correct and independently proven via the Lane C parity runs (0 drift across 141 rows) and the live compiled-route-status probe; this is stale TEST EXPECTATIONS, not a routing bug.
findings.distinctFrom: This is NOT the same as the already-documented advisor-side DEFAULT_ON_HUBS gap (see findings.advisorSideCohortStillEmpty) -- that one concerns a third, intentionally-separate copy used only for advisor recommend-enrichment and is explicitly disclosed as a known non-blocking follow-up in the packet's own handover.md/implementation-summary.md. This finding is about a *test file* for the *main runtime resolver* itself, not previously called out in the docs I could find.
findings.relatedLikelyFailure: grep-confirmed (not executed, to avoid re-triggering the compiled-route-sync.cjs mutation risk -- see incidents[0]): .opencode/bin/tests/compiled-route-manifest.test.cjs line 489 `assert.equal(resolver.DEFAULT_ON_HUBS.size, 0)` requires the SAME resolve.cjs module already directly confirmed to have .size===7, so this assertion is deterministically guaranteed to also fail if the file is run via `node --test`. Reported as high-confidence-inferred, not executed.
findings.notPartOfMandatedSuites: Neither file is part of the required 18-file/258-test skill-benchmark suite or the 47-test compiled-routing-parity vitest; both live under a separate .opencode/vitest.config.bin.ts config, found via the task's item-6 tree search.
findings.action: None taken (verification-only task, and these are test files, not routing/manifest/DEFAULT_ON_HUBS source -- editing them would be outside the explicit read-only/no-mutation scope of this task). Recommend a follow-up packet updates these 2 files' assertions to match the post-7dfffa0c93 default-on cohort.

findings.title: DEFAULT_ON_HUBS exists in 3 places; the 2 that matter for actual serving are populated with all 7 hubs and byte-identical; the 3rd (advisor enrichment only) is a known, already-documented, non-blocking empty follow-up

direct Read; `diff` against the promoted copy above returned exit 0 (byte-identical)

copies.path: .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp-server/lib/compiled-routing-flag.ts (+ dist mirror .js) -- advisor recommend-enrichment cohort only
copies.confirmedBy: direct Read; matches the packet's own implementation-summary.md line 207/219 and handover.md line 54, which already disclose this exact gap as a known, non-blocking follow-up ('unaffected by this cutover')

findings.note: My initial instinct on finding the empty advisor-side copy (before locating the other two) was to treat this as a LOGIC-SYNC contradiction against the task's 'default-on for all 7 hubs' premise. Broadening the search (my first grep was scoped only to .opencode/skills and missed .opencode/bin) resolved this: the task's premise is accurate for the resolver that actually serves routing; the advisor-enrichment copy is a separate, smaller, already-tracked concern. No escalation needed; reported here for full transparency of the investigation path.

incidents.severity: resolved, fully reverted, zero residual impact
incidents.whatHappened: Ran `node .opencode/bin/compiled-route-status.cjs --help` (confirmed read-only via source, printed a clean usage line, exit 0), then `node .opencode/bin/compiled-route-sync.cjs --help` to probe its interface the same way. compiled-route-sync.cjs's main() only recognizes --verify and --check; any other argv (including --help) falls through to the default build() action, which DELETES and re-promotes the entire .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/ closure from the authored spec-tree source. This is a real mutator, not a no-op.
incidents.detection: Immediate: the command's own stdout read 'promoted 65 closure files -> .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing' instead of a usage message. Followed up with `git status`/`git diff --stat`, which showed 19 tracked files modified: 18 under .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/** (canary-router.cjs, registry-compiler.cjs, router.cjs, build-artifacts.cjs, compiled-route.cjs, and all 7 hubs' activation manifest.json policy-hash fields) plus .opencode/skills/sk-doc/create-skill/references/parent-skill/compiled-routing-lockstep-surfaces.json.
incidents.resolution: `git checkout -- .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/` followed by `git checkout -- .opencode/skills/sk-doc/create-skill/references/parent-skill/compiled-routing-lockstep-surfaces.json`. Verified `git status --short` returned to the exact 7-line pre-incident baseline, and the 3 frozen scorer file SHA-256 hashes (which this incident never touched) were re-confirmed identical before/after.
incidents.resolveCjsNotTouched: Critically, .../011-runtime-engine/lib/resolve.cjs (the file holding DEFAULT_ON_HUBS) was NOT among the 19 changed files -- confirmed by the diff --stat output. DEFAULT_ON_HUBS was never at risk from this incident.
incidents.sideObservation: The rebuild's diff (before revert) showed several OTHER promoted files -- registry-compiler.cjs, router.cjs, canary-router.cjs, build-artifacts.cjs, and per-hub manifest.json generation-hash fields -- differing between the currently-committed 'promoted' copy and the current 'authored' spec-tree copy. This suggests a small pre-existing (not caused by this session) authored-vs-promoted drift for non-resolver files that predates this task. Not investigated further (outside task scope); noted for awareness only. resolve.cjs itself (the DEFAULT_ON_HUBS holder) was confirmed byte-identical between authored and promoted both before and after this incident.
incidents.postIncidentSafeguard: For the remainder of the session, no script was executed without first reading its source to confirm read-only behavior (compiled-route-status.cjs, compiled-route-sync.cjs --verify, render-serving-snapshot.cjs --validate were all vetted this way before execution).

constraintCompliance.gitStatusNote: These are exactly the 2 pre-existing 'strays' this task was told to never touch -- both were already dirty at session start and were never opened or edited by this session. 5 OTHER files that were dirty at session start (013-compiled-coverage-buildout's graph-metadata.json/implementation-summary.md/plan.md/spec.md + goal-coverage-buildout.md) are no longer dirty because a CONCURRENT session/agent on the same worktree committed them as `2eaac22e95` during this session's runtime -- not an action taken by this session (zero `git commit` invocations occurred in this session).
